Congressional Research Service — R48012

The BIOSECURE Act of 2025: Overview and Implementation Status
Updated January 15, 2026

SUMMARY

The BIOSECURE Act (P.L. 119-7) was signed into law on December 3, 2025. The Act prohibits federal agencies and federal contractors from procuring biotechnology services and equipment from five named companies: WuXi AppTec (including WuXi STA and WuXi Biologics), BGI Genomics, MGI Tech, Complete Genomics, and Mindray.

The prohibition applies to new contracts immediately upon enactment, and to existing contracts with a phase-out deadline of January 1, 2027. Any company receiving federal grants, contracts, or participation in federal healthcare programs (including Medicare/Medicaid) is covered.

CURRENT STATUS: Implementation is proceeding. Federal agencies are auditing contracts for covered company exposure. No regulatory guidance has been issued yet by OMB. The Act is in effect as enacted.

INDUSTRY IMPACT: The pharmaceutical contract manufacturing industry is significantly affected. WuXi STA provides GMP small molecule synthesis services for hundreds of pharmaceutical products. Industry associations estimate 10-15% of US small-molecule API manufacturing involves at least one WuXi STA step.

This CRS report summarizes the law as enacted (P.L. 119-7, signed December 3, 2025). It does not contain new information beyond what was in the legislation itself. For the full text of the Act, see congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/7085.
